content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

March 8, 1990

John Lawson
City Attorney
1600 Huntington Drive
Duarte, CA 91010

Re: Your Request for Informal Assistance
Our File No. I-90-073

Dear Mr. Lawson:

You are seeking advice on behalf of the City of Duarte
councilmember Jim Kirchner regarding his duties and responsibili-
ties under the conflict-of-interest provisions of the Political
Reform Act (the "Act").1 As Mr. Kirchner's authorized representa-
tive, we provide you with advice pursuant to Regulation 18329(b),
but as your request for advice does not refer to a specific go-
vernmental decision, we are treating your question as a request
for informal assistance only pursuant to Regulation 18329 (copy
enclosed).2

The following advice is based upon the facts provided in your
letter of January 23, 1990, as supplemented by subsequent tele-
phone conversations with you.

QUESTION

Councilmember Kirchner is the president of the city's chamber
of commerce. Because the city contracts with the chamber for va-
rious services and pays for the services out of the city's general

1 Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory references
are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. Commission
regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations Section
18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title 2,
Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

2 Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the
immunity provided by an opinion or formal written advice. (Sec-
tion 83114; Regulation 18329(c)(3).)

428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

File No. I-90-067
Page 2

fund, does a conflict of interest exist for Mr. Kirchner that requires his disqualification from participation in decisions involving the chamber?

CONCLUSION

Mr. Kirchner may participate in council decisions to which the chamber of commerce is a party.

FACTS

Jim Kirchner is an elected member of the city council of the City of Duarte (the "city"). Mr. Kirchner is also president of the Duarte Chamber of Commerce (the "chamber"), a nonprofit entity. The city contracts with, and from the general fund pays, the chamber for miscellaneous services. As part of the city's annual budget process, the city council determines the scope of these services and the payments for them: the services and the appropriate payments are included as part of the council-approved city budget.

Mr. Kirchner receives no salary or other monetary compensation from the chamber for his services as president. He is also not reimbursed for any expenses he incurs resulting from his participation in chamber activities. His employment is located outside of the city and his employer is not a member of the chamber.

ANALYSIS

The Act prohibits a public official from making, participating in, or using his official position to influence a governmental decision in which he knows or has reason to know he has a financial interest. (Section 87100.) Mr. Kirchner is a public official because he is an elected member of the Duarte City Council (Section 82048). He must therefore disqualify himself from any city council decision in which he is financially interested.

Section 87103 states that a public official is financially interested in a decision when it is reasonably foreseeable that the decision will have a material financial effect, distinguishable from its effect on the public generally, on the official or a member of his immediate family, or, among other things,

(c) Any source of income ... aggregating two hundred fifty dollars ($250) or more in value provided to, received by or promised to the public official within 12 months prior to the time when the decision is made.

3 Chambers of commerce in California are nonprofit "business and trade association" entities under Internal Revenue Code Section 501(c)(6).

C943

File No. I-90-067
Page 3

(d) Any business entity in which the public
official is a director, officer, partner, trustee,
employee, or holds any position of management.

Section 87103 (c) and (d).

You have indicated that Mr. Kirchner receives no salary for
his services as the president of the chamber. Further, as president of the chamber he is not an officer of a "business entity,"
since the chamber is not a business entity for purposes of the
Act.4 Therefore, there does not appear to be any economic interest in the chamber which would serve as the basis for Mr. Kirchner's disqualification from council decisions involving the chamber.
(See Lyon Advice Letter, A-88-391, copy enclosed.) Our advice would be different if Mr. Kirchner were compensated for his
services as president in an amount which met the requisite threshold as specified in Section 87103(c). (See Flitner, A-84-009,
copy enclosed.)5

I trust this letter has provided you with the guidance you
requested. If you have any further questions regarding this matter please contact me at (916) 322-5091.

Sincerely,

Kathryn E. Donovan
General Counsel

By: Jonathan S. Rothman
Counsel, Legal Division

KED:JSR:plh

Enclosures

___________________________

4 Section 82005 defines "business entity" as "an organization or
enterprise operated for profit, including but not limited to a
proprietorship, partnership, firm, business trust, joint venture,
syndicate, corporation or association."

5 In that case, Regulation 18702.5 (copy enclosed) would apply to
determine whether the effect of a decision on the chamber is material.

sections.analysis:
The Act prohibits a public official from making, participating in, or using his official position to influence a governmental decision in which he knows or has reason to know he has a financial interest. (Section 87100.) Mr. Kirchner is a public official because he is an elected member of the Duarte City Council (Section 82048). Section 87103 states that a public official is financially interested in a decision when it is reasonably foreseeable that the decision will have a material financial effect, distinguishable from its effect on the public generally, on the official or a member of his immediate family, or, among other things,

(c) Any source of income ... aggregating two hundred fifty dollars ($250) or more in value provided to, received by or promised to the public official within 12 months prior to the time when the decision is made.

3 Chambers of commerce in California are nonprofit "business and trade association" entities under Internal Revenue Code Section 501(C)(6).

(d) Any business entity in which the public official is a director, officer, partner, trustee, employee, or holds any position of management.

Section 87103 (c) and (d).

You have indicated that Mr. Kirchner receives no salary for his services as the president of the chamber. Further, as president of the chamber he is not an officer of a "business entity," since the chamber is not a business entity for purposes of the Act.4 Therefore, there does not appear to be any economic interest in the chamber which would serve as the basis for Mr. Kirchner's disqualification from council decisions involving the chamber. (See Flitner, A-84-009, copy enclosed.)5

I trust this letter has provided you with the guidance you requested. If you have any further questions regarding this matter please contact me at (916) 322-5091.
